headline: Sarawak Immigration Control Tests Federal Boundaries
context: Sarawak exercised immigration autonomy to deny entry to 14,000 Peninsula Malaysians in 2025 for failing to meet new state visa requirements.

cards.big: Sarawak denied entry to 14,000 Peninsula Malaysians in 2025
cards.sub: New state visa requirements provoked federal backlash and constitutional debate

cards.big: Immigration autonomy under MA63 restored via constitutional amendment in 2022
cards.sub: Sarawak now requires work permits for non-Sarawakians staying beyond 90 days

cards.big: Tourism Ministry estimated RM180 million in lost revenue from stricter rules
cards.sub: Hotels in Kuching reported 15% drop in Peninsula Malaysian visitors late 2025

cards.big: Federal AG office questioned whether denials breach Article 8 equality provisions
cards.sub: Sarawak argued MA63 predates and supersedes uniformity provisions of constitution

cards.big: Immigration autonomy tests whether MA63 rights can be exercised without penalty
Immigration control is Sarawak's sharpest autonomy tool — and the one Putrajaya most resists.
